there's lots of prehistoric toothed whale skeletons in Egypt, which when you look at them do resemble giant crocodile serpents.

In the original Canaanite tellings of the Baal Cycle (which predate Christianity by centuries), Tannin was a primordial sea monster which represented chaos (sound familiar?) and a fellow servant of Yam alongside Lotan. In Judaism and subsequent branches, the name becomes more of a "category" of monsters (in which Leviathan is included) created during Genesis rather than a single unit.

While tannin in modern Hebrew does refer to crocodiles, the original tanninim seemed to cover the whole gamut from sea monsters to land serpents and the word is often used to refer to the latter. In most notably the Greek Septaguint and the English KJV however, both the tanninim created during Genesis before humanity itself and the tannin that swallows Joseph Jonah are very specifically sea creatures and referred to directly as whales in translation (ceta in the Septaguint's case, tying in the connection to the Greek sea monster of the same name that is immortalized as a whale constellation, hence her WoD).
don't forget that the whale remains are also scattered across jungles and such, also beaches (beaching behavior), also ancient seabeds
hence a whale carcass is found in a valley/canyon (could be ancient seabed) and covered in silver (Lotan's silver)
